evals.prompt: I need to archive a Telegram channel I already follow into SQLite. Please set up a reusable Telethon workflow that reads credentials from .env, stores media on disk, and tells me what to put in the .env file if it is missing.
evals.expected_output: Uses the bundled telegram-channel-sync script, provides the required .env keys including my.telegram.org guidance, and avoids inventing a new scraper.

evals.prompt: Sync this private Telegram channel invite safely: https://t.me/+AbCdEf123456. I do not want my account rate-limited or banned. I want SQLite plus downloaded photos and videos.
evals.expected_output: Explains that private channel import is explicit, uses TG_JOIN_INVITE and TG_INVITE_LINK only when authorized, keeps serial/conservative sync defaults, and stores media paths in SQLite.

evals.prompt: My .env is empty and I ran the Telegram sync. What should the tool print, and where do I get TG_API_ID and TG_API_HASH?
evals.expected_output: Shows the missing-key checklist, mentions https://my.telegram.org -> API Development tools, and explains first login/session reuse.

evals.prompt: I need to backfill an entire Telegram channel with no date limit, then rerun later to only collect new messages. Use Telethon and SQLite and make reruns safe.
evals.expected_output: Uses iter_messages(limit=None), SQLite sync_state with newest and oldest checkpoints, and idempotent upserts for resumable all-time backfill plus incremental sync.

evals.prompt: The Telegram channel has voice messages. Transcribe them if Telegram supports it, but do not use OpenAI or Whisper. If Premium is required, keep syncing and record the failure.
evals.expected_output: Uses Telethon messages.TranscribeAudioRequest for voice messages only, records complete/pending/premium_required/failed statuses, and does not add external STT services.
